Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Auto Row Parts, And C.B. Investment Co.
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Auto Row Parts and C.B. Investment Co.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Rikus Linschoten, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Peter Murchison, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Auto Row Parts or C.B. Investment Co.
Plaintiff's complaint was filed on October 27, 2014 and served on November 17, 2014. Proof of Service was filed November 26, 2014.
Statement of Damages was served on defendant C.B. Investment Co. on November 17, 2014 and filed with the Clerk's Office on November 26, 2014.
Default entered as to defendant C.B. Investment Co. on January 28, 2015.
DOE Amendment substituting Auto Row Parts for DOE 8 was filed on October 28, 2015.
Plaintiff's complaint and Statement of Damages were served on defendant Auto Row Parts on November 11, 2015. Proof of Service was filed with the Clerk's Office on November 24, 2015.
Order Striking defendant Auto Row Parts' answer was filed on May 25, 2018.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Auto Row Parts was filed on June 5, 2018.
Default entered as to defendant Auto Row Parts on June 5, 2018.
Remaining DOE defendants were dismissed on June 13, 2018.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on June 21, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Peter Murchison, and against the Defaulted Defendants Auto Row Parts and C.B. Investment Co., as follows:

Defendant Auto Row Parts:
Economic Damages - $73,250.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $750,000.00.
Total - $823,250.00.

Defendant E.B. Investment Co:
Economic Damages - $73,250.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $750,000.00.
Total - $823,250.00.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-23-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Nevin Brownfield, Esq. of ONGARO PC (415.433.3900), appearing for defendant / moving party Auto Row Parts Co.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq. of BRAYTON PURCELL LLP (415.898.1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing party Peter Murchison.

RULING: Defendant Auto Row Parts Co.'s Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Auto Row Parts Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of conclusively negating an element of plaintiff's cause of action pursuant to Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, which is the sole way defendant has attempted to sustain its burden. The evidence submitted by defendant - the declarations of Jerrold Cresta, Robert Giannini, and Nevin Brownfield, the Secretary of State detail, and the various correspondence with insurance companies - is insufficient to demonstrate that defendant does not have undistributed assets, including any insurance assets held by the corporation that may be available to satisfy claims. (Corp. Code 2011(a)(1)(A).) Plaintiff's objections to the declarations of Jerrold Cresta and Robert Giannini are overruled. The Court did not consider defendant's reply evidence. (Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243; San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Court Reporter: Beth Grant of AikenWelch, CSR #10943 (Tel. 707.888.3689). (503/CML)
